subject: Re: processing tilt series with different dose
body:
Hi MrJ,

In principle it should be fine. The only place where the dose *really*
matters in warp (afaik) is at the `import tilt series from imod` step. If
you want doses to be *correct*, you can make sure that the mdoc files have
the right doses in the right fields (you can edit them yourself if they are
not already good), and you should see the correct doses popping up when
importing. Otherwise, you can provide manually a dose for all tilt series,
which might or might not cause problems. I would say differences are
probably negligible for your resolution target and if the total doses per
TS are within 10-20e of each other? Just eyeballing here though, I don't
have hard numbers sorry. I would personally ensure to have correct doses on
my mdocs :P

Best,
Lorenzo

On Tue, Jun 27, 2023 at 11:52 AM mrJ <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> hi all,
> is it possible to process different tilt series which have different total
> dose?  Probably not very straightforward.  What will be the outcome if I
> use the same dose for all?  I am not aiming for high resolution here, it's
> a large particle and I am hoping for 10-15A.
> thanks
> J
>
